| `sum-range-pow-four-triangular-form` вАФ For every natural n, 15¬Ј(sum of iвБі for i in 0..n) = (sum of i for i in 0..n)¬Ј(2n+1)¬Ј(3n¬≤+3nвИТ1); i.e. вИСkвБі = T(2n+1)(3n¬≤+3nвИТ1)/15 where T = вИСk. | proved | 3 | вАФ | classic identities (Faulhaber-in-T tower вАФ even-power rung) | Faulhaber's theorem, even-power case: вИСkвБі = n(n+1)(2n+1)(3n¬≤+3nвИТ1)/30 = T(2n+1)(3n¬≤+3nвИТ1)/15. Knuth, "Johann Faulhaber and sums of powers", Math. Comp. 61 (1993); CRC Standard Mathematical Tables. |
| `sum-range-pow-seven-closed-form` вАФ For every natural n, 24¬Ј(sum of iвБЈ for i in 0..n) = n¬≤(n+1)¬≤(3nвБі+6n¬≥вИТn¬≤вИТ4n+2); Faulhaber's closed form for seventh powers, вИСkвБЈ = (3nвБЄ+12nвБЈ+14nвБґвИТ7nвБі+2n¬≤)/24. | proved | 4 | вАФ | classic identities (power-sum tower вАФ the harder odd-power rung) | Faulhaber's formula, p = 7; Conway & Guy, The Book of Numbers; D. E. Knuth, "Johann Faulhaber and sums of powers", Math. Comp. 61 (1993). Faulhaber's own result: odd-power sums are polynomials in the triangular number T = n(n+1)/2. |
| `sum-range-pow-seven-faulhaber-triangular` вАФ For every natural n, 3¬Ј(sum of iвБЈ for i in 0..n) = (sum of i for i in 0..n)¬≤¬Ј(6¬Ј(sum of i for i in 0..n)¬≤вИТ4¬Ј(sum of i for i in 0..n)+1); i.e. вИСkвБЈ = T¬≤(6T¬≤вИТ4T+1)/3 where T = вИСk. Knuth, "Johann Faulhaber and sums of powers", Math. Comp. 61 (1993). |
| `sum-range-pow-six-closed-form` вАФ For every natural n, 42¬Ј(sum of iвБґ for i in 0..n) = n(n+1)(2n+1)(3nвБі+6n¬≥вИТ3n+1); Faulhaber's closed form for sixth powers, вИСkвБґ = (6nвБЈ+21nвБґ+21nвБµвИТ7n¬≥+n)/42. | proved | 3 | вАФ | classic identities (power-sum tower вАФ the next rung above proved p=2..p=5) | Faulhaber's formula, p = 6; Conway & Guy, The Book of Numbers (sums of powers); D. E. Knuth, "Johann Faulhaber and sums of powers", Math. Comp. 61 (1993); CRC Standard Mathematical Tables. |
| `sum-range-pronic` вАФ For every natural n, 3 * (sum of i(i+1) for i in 0..n) = n(n+1)(n+2); the sum of the first pronic (oblong) numbers, equivalently 2 * C(n+2, 3). | proved | 2 | вАФ | classic identities | Pronic/oblong number sums вИСk(k+1) = n(n+1)(n+2)/3; Graham, Knuth & Patashnik, Concrete Mathematics, Ch. 2; CRC Standard Mathematical Tables (figurate numbers). |
| `sum-range-recip-pronic` вАФ For every natural n, the sum over i in 0..n-1 of 1/((i+1)(i+2)) equals n/(n+1) (over вДЪ); the classic telescoping sum 1/(1¬Ј2) + 1/(2¬Ј3) + ... + 1/(n(n+1)) = n/(n+1). | proved | 3 | вАФ | classic identities | Classic telescoping/partial-fractions identity вИС 1/(k(k+1)) = n/(n+1); Graham, Knuth & Patashnik, Concrete Mathematics, ¬І2.5 (partial fractions); standard first-year analysis exercise. |
| `sum-range-recip-triangular` вАФ For every natural n, вИС_{k<n} 2/((k+1)(k+2)) = 2n/(n+1) over вДЪ: the reciprocals of the triangular numbers telescope (вИС 1/T_k вЖТ 2). | proved | 3 | вАФ | telescoping series (reciprocals of triangular numbers) | Standard telescoping series; the sum of reciprocals of all triangular numbers is 2. | `sum-range-sq-even` вАФ For every natural n, 3 * (sum of (2i)^2 for i in 0..n-1) = 2n(n-1)(2n-1); the sum of the squares of the first n even numbers 0^2 + 2^2 + ... + (2n-2)^2 in closed form. | proved | 2 | вАФ | classic identities | Even-square sums вИС(2k)^2 = 2m(m+1)(2m+1)/3 (here reindexed from 0); CRC Standard Mathematical Tables (sums of powers); companion of the proved sum-range-sq-odd-closed-form. |

| `sum-range-sq-triangular-form` вАФ For every natural n, 3¬Ј(sum of i¬≤ for i in 0..n) = (sum of i for i in 0..n)¬Ј(2n+1); i.e. вИСk¬≤ = T¬Ј(2n+1)/3 where T = вИСk = n(n+1)/2 is the n-th triangular number. | proved | 2 | вАФ | classic identities (Faulhaber-in-T tower вАФ power sums as polynomials in the triangular number) | Faulhaber's theorem: вИСkбµЦ is a polynomial in T = n(n+1)/2; the even-power cases carry a factor (2n+1). вИСk¬≤ = n(n+1)(2n+1)/6 = T(2n+1)/3.
